english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Hebrews

verses.latin_vulgate: Habuit quidem et prius justificationes culturæ, et Sanctum sæculare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶχέν μὲν οὖν καὶ ἡ πρώτη σκηνή δικαιώματα λατρείας τό τε ἅγιον κοσμικόν
verses.king_james: Then verily the first *covenant* had also ordinances of divine service, and a worldly sanctuary.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tabernaculum enim factum est primum, in quo erant candelabra, et mensa, et propositio panum, quæ dicitur Sancta.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σκηνὴ γὰρ κατεσκευάσθη ἡ πρώτη ἐν ᾗ ἥ τε λυχνία καὶ ἡ τράπεζα καὶ ἡ πρόθεσις τῶν ἄρτων ἥτις λέγεται ἅγια
verses.king_james: For there was a tabernacle made; the first, wherein *was* the candlestick, and the table, and the shewbread; which is called the sanctuary.

verses.latin_vulgate: Post velamentum autem secundum, tabernaculum, quod dicitur Sancta sanctorum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ δὲ τὸ δεύτερον καταπέτασμα σκηνὴ ἡ λεγομένη ἅγια ἁγίων
verses.king_james: And after the second veil, the tabernacle which is called the Holiest of all;

verses.latin_vulgate: aureum habens thuribulum, et arcam testamenti circumtectam ex omni parte auro, in qua urna aurea habens manna, et virga Aaron, quæ fronduerat, et tabulæ testamenti,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: χρυσοῦν ἔχουσα θυμιατήριον καὶ τὴν κιβωτὸν τῆς διαθήκης περικεκαλυμμένην πάντοθεν χρυσίῳ ἐν ᾗ στάμνος χρυσῆ ἔχουσα τὸ μάννα καὶ ἡ ῥάβδος ἀαρὼν ἡ βλαστήσασα καὶ αἱ πλάκες τῆς διαθήκης
verses.king_james: Which had the golden censer, and the ark of the covenant overlaid round about with gold, wherein *was* he golden pot that had manna, and Aaron's rod that budded, and the tables of the covenant;

verses.latin_vulgate: superque eam erant cherubim gloriæ obumbrantia propitiatorium: de quibus non est modo dicendum per singula.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑπεράνω δὲ αὐτῆς χερουβιμ δόξης κατασκιάζοντα τὸ ἱλαστήριον περὶ ὧν οὐκ ἔστιν νῦν λέγειν κατὰ μέρος
verses.king_james: And over it the cherubims of glory shadowing the mercyseat; of which we cannot now speak particularly.

verses.latin_vulgate: His vero ita compositis, in priori quidem tabernaculo semper introibant sacerdotes, sacrificiorum officia consummantes:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τούτων δὲ οὕτως κατεσκευασμένων εἰς μὲν τὴν πρώτην σκηνὴν διαπαντός εἰσίασιν οἱ ἱερεῖς τὰς λατρείας ἐπιτελοῦντες
verses.king_james: Now when these things were thus ordained, the priests went always into the first tabernacle, accomplishing the service *of God*.

verses.latin_vulgate: in secundo autem semel in anno solus pontifex non sine sanguine, quem offert pro sua et populi ignorantia:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰς δὲ τὴν δευτέραν ἅπαξ τοῦ ἐνιαυτοῦ μόνος ὁ ἀρχιερεύς οὐ χωρὶς αἵματος ὃ προσφέρει ὑπὲρ ἑαυτοῦ καὶ τῶν τοῦ λαοῦ ἀγνοημάτων
verses.king_james: But into the second *went* the high priest alone once every year, not without blood, which he offered for himself, and *for* the errors of the people:

verses.latin_vulgate: hoc significante Spiritu Sancto, nondum propalatam esse sanctorum viam, adhuc priore tabernaculo habente statum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦτο δηλοῦντος τοῦ πνεύματος τοῦ ἁγίου μήπω πεφανερῶσθαι τὴν τῶν ἁγίων ὁδὸν ἔτι τῆς πρώτης σκηνῆς ἐχούσης στάσιν
verses.king_james: The Holy Ghost this signifying, that the way into the holiest of all was not yet made manifest, while as the first tabernacle was yet standing:

verses.latin_vulgate: quæ parabola est temporis instantis: juxta quam munera, et hostiæ offeruntur, quæ non possunt juxta conscientiam perfectum facere servientem, solummodo in cibis, et in potibus,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἥτις παραβολὴ εἰς τὸν καιρὸν τὸν ἐνεστηκότα καθ᾽ ὃν δῶρά τε καὶ θυσίαι προσφέρονται μὴ δυνάμεναι κατὰ συνείδησιν τελειῶσαι τὸν λατρεύοντα
verses.king_james: Which *was* a figure for the time then present, in which were offered both gifts and sacrifices, that could not make him that did the service perfect, as pertaining to the conscience;

verses.latin_vulgate: et variis baptismatibus, et justitiis carnis usque ad tempus correctionis impositis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μόνον ἐπὶ βρώμασιν καὶ πόμασιν καὶ διαφόροις βαπτισμοῖς καὶ δικαιώμασιν σαρκὸς μέχρι καιροῦ διορθώσεως ἐπικείμενα
verses.king_james: *Which stood* only in meats and drinks, and divers washings, and carnal ordinances, imposed *on them* until the time of reformation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Christus autem assistens pontifex futurorum bonorum, per amplius et perfectius tabernaculum, non manufactum, id est, non hujus creationis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: χριστὸς δὲ παραγενόμενος ἀρχιερεὺς τῶν μελλόντων ἀγαθῶν διὰ τῆς μείζονος καὶ τελειοτέρας σκηνῆς οὐ χειροποιήτου τουτέστιν οὐ ταύτης τῆς κτίσεως
verses.king_james: But Christ being come an high priest of good things to come, by a greater and more perfect tabernacle, not made with hands, that is to say, not of this building;

verses.latin_vulgate: neque per sanguinem hircorum aut vitulorum, sed per proprium sanguinem introivit semel in Sancta, æterna redemptione inventa.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐδὲ δι᾽ αἵματος τράγων καὶ μόσχων διὰ δὲ τοῦ ἰδίου αἵματος εἰσῆλθεν ἐφάπαξ εἰς τὰ ἅγια αἰωνίαν λύτρωσιν εὑράμενος
verses.king_james: Neither by the blood of goats and calves, but by his own blood he entered in once into the holy place, having obtained eternal redemption *for us*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si enim sanguis hircorum et taurorum, et cinis vitulæ aspersus inquinatos sanctificat ad emundationem carnis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ γὰρ τὸ αἷμα ταύρων καὶ τράγων καὶ σποδὸς δαμάλεως ῥαντίζουσα τοὺς κεκοινωμένους ἁγιάζει πρὸς τὴν τῆς σαρκὸς καθαρότητα
verses.king_james: For if the blood of bulls and of goats, and the ashes of an heifer sprinkling the unclean, sanctifieth to the purifying of the flesh:

verses.latin_vulgate: quanto magis sanguis Christi, qui per Spiritum Sanctum semetipsum obtulit immaculatum Deo, emundabit conscientiam nostram ab operibus mortuis, ad serviendum Deo viventi?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πόσῳ μᾶλλον τὸ αἷμα τοῦ χριστοῦ ὃς διὰ πνεύματος αἰωνίου ἑαυτὸν προσήνεγκεν ἄμωμον τῷ θεῷ καθαριεῖ τὴν συνείδησιν ὑμῶν ἀπὸ νεκρῶν ἔργων εἰς τὸ λατρεύειν θεῷ ζῶντι
verses.king_james: How much more shall the blood of Christ, who through the eternal Spirit offered himself without spot to God, purge your conscience from dead works to serve the living God?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ideo novi testamenti mediator est: ut morte intercedente, in redemptionem earum prævaricationum, quæ erant sub priori testamento, repromissionem accipiant qui vocati sunt æternæ hæreditatis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ διὰ τοῦτο διαθήκης καινῆς μεσίτης ἐστίν, ὅπως θανάτου γενομένου εἰς ἀπολύτρωσιν τῶν ἐπὶ τῇ πρώτῃ διαθήκῃ παραβάσεων τὴν ἐπαγγελίαν λάβωσιν οἱ κεκλημένοι τῆς αἰωνίου κληρονομίας
verses.king_james: And for this cause he is the mediator of the new testament, that by means of death, for the redemption of the transgressions *that were* under the first testament, they which are called might receive the promise of eternal inheritance.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ubi enim testamentum est, mors necesse est intercedat testatoris.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅπου γὰρ διαθήκη θάνατον ἀνάγκη φέρεσθαι τοῦ διαθεμένου
verses.king_james: For where a testament *is*, there must also of necessity be the death of the testator.

verses.latin_vulgate: Testamentum enim in mortuis confirmatum est: alioquin nondum valet, dum vivit qui testatus est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διαθήκη γὰρ ἐπὶ νεκροῖς βεβαία ἐπεὶ μήποτε ἰσχύει ὅτε ζῇ ὁ διαθέμενος
verses.king_james: For a testament *is* of force after men are dead: otherwise it is of no strength at all while the testator liveth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Unde nec primum quidem sine sanguine dedicatum est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅθεν οὐδ᾽ ἡ πρώτη χωρὶς αἵματος ἐγκεκαίνισται
verses.king_james: Whereupon neither the first *testament* was dedicated without blood.

verses.latin_vulgate: Lecto enim omni mandato legis a Moyse universo populo, accipiens sanguinem vitulorum et hircorum cum aqua, et lana coccinea, et hyssopo, ipsum quoque librum, et omnem populum aspersit,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λαληθείσης γὰρ πάσης ἐντολῆς κατὰ νόμον ὑπὸ μωϋσέως παντὶ τῷ λαῷ λαβὼν τὸ αἷμα τῶν μόσχων καὶ τράγων μετὰ ὕδατος καὶ ἐρίου κοκκίνου καὶ ὑσσώπου αὐτό τε τὸ βιβλίον καὶ πάντα τὸν λαὸν ἐῤῥάντισεν
verses.king_james: For when Moses had spoken every precept to all the people according to the law, he took the blood of calves and of goats, with water, and scarlet wool, and hyssop, and sprinkled both the book, and all the people,

verses.latin_vulgate: dicens: Hic sanguis testamenti, quod mandavit ad vos Deus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγων τοῦτο τὸ αἷμα τῆς διαθήκης ἡς ἐνετείλατο πρὸς ὑμᾶς ὁ θεός
verses.king_james: Saying, This *is* the blood of the testament which God hath enjoined unto you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Etiam tabernaculum et omnia vasa ministerii sanguine similiter aspersit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τὴν σκηνὴν δὲ καὶ πάντα τὰ σκεύη τῆς λειτουργίας τῷ αἵματι ὁμοίως ἐῤῥάντισεν
verses.king_james: Moreover he sprinkled with blood both the tabernacle, and all the vessels of the ministry.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et omnia pene in sanguine secundum legem mundantur: et sine sanguinis effusione non fit remissio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ σχεδὸν ἐν αἵματι πάντα καθαρίζεται κατὰ τὸν νόμον καὶ χωρὶς αἱματεκχυσίας οὐ γίνεται ἄφεσις
verses.king_james: And almost all things are by the law purged with blood; and without shedding of blood is no remission.

verses.latin_vulgate: Necesse est ergo exemplaria quidem cælestium his mundari: ipsa autem cælestia melioribus hostiis quam istis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀνάγκη οὖν τὰ μὲν ὑποδείγματα τῶν ἐν τοῖς οὐρανοῖς τούτοις καθαρίζεσθαι αὐτὰ δὲ τὰ ἐπουράνια κρείττοσιν θυσίαις παρὰ ταύτας
verses.king_james: *It was* therefore necessary that the patterns of things in the heavens should be purified with these; but the heavenly things themselves with better sacrifices than these.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim in manufacta Sancta Jesus introivit exemplaria verorum: sed in ipsum cælum, ut appareat nunc vultui Dei pro nobis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ γὰρ εἰς χειροποίητα ἅγια εἰσῆλθεν ὁ χριστός ἀντίτυπα τῶν ἀληθινῶν ἀλλ᾽ εἰς αὐτὸν τὸν οὐρανόν νῦν ἐμφανισθῆναι τῷ προσώπῳ τοῦ θεοῦ ὑπὲρ ἡμῶν
verses.king_james: For Christ is not entered into the holy places made with hands, *which are* the figures of the true; but into heaven itself, now to appear in the presence of God for us:

verses.latin_vulgate: neque ut sæpe offerat semetipsum, quemadmodum pontifex intrat in Sancta per singulos annos in sanguine alieno:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐδ᾽ ἵνα πολλάκις προσφέρῃ ἑαυτόν ὥσπερ ὁ ἀρχιερεὺς εἰσέρχεται εἰς τὰ ἅγια κατ᾽ ἐνιαυτὸν ἐν αἵματι ἀλλοτρίῳ
verses.king_james: Nor yet that he should offer himself often, as the high priest entereth into the holy place every year with blood of others;

verses.latin_vulgate: alioquin oportebat eum frequenter pati ab origine mundi: nunc autem semel in consummatione sæculorum, ad destitutionem peccati, per hostiam suam apparuit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπεὶ ἔδει αὐτὸν πολλάκις παθεῖν ἀπὸ καταβολῆς κόσμου νῦν δὲ ἅπαξ ἐπὶ συντελείᾳ τῶν αἰώνων εἰς ἀθέτησιν ἁμαρτίας διὰ τῆς θυσίας αὐτοῦ πεφανέρωται
verses.king_james: For then must he often have suffered since the foundation of the world: but now once in the end of the world hath he appeared to put away sin by the sacrifice of himself.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et quemadmodum statutum est hominibus semel mori, post hoc autem judicium:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ καθ᾽ ὅσον ἀπόκειται τοῖς ἀνθρώποις ἅπαξ ἀποθανεῖν μετὰ δὲ τοῦτο κρίσις
verses.king_james: And as it is appointed unto men once to die, but after this the judgment:

verses.latin_vulgate: sic et Christus semel oblatus est ad multorum exhaurienda peccata: secundo sine peccato apparebit exspectantibus se, in salutem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὕτως ὁ χριστός ἅπαξ προσενεχθεὶς εἰς τὸ πολλῶν ἀνενεγκεῖν ἁμαρτίας ἐκ δευτέρου χωρὶς ἁμαρτίας ὀφθήσεται τοῖς αὐτὸν ἀπεκδεχομένοις εἰς σωτηρίαν
verses.king_james: So Christ was once offered to bear the sins of many; and unto them that look for him shall he appear the second time without sin unto salvation.
